title: Reading - Part 1
instructions: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.heading: Film Club
questions.text: This month’s film club choice is a comedy made by local students. The director will answer questions afterwards. Tickets are free, but you must collect one from the office because seats are limited.
questions.question: What does the poster tell people?

options.C: They need a ticket even though there is no charge.
options.A: They can ask local students to act in a film.
options.B: They must pay at the office before the film.

questions.explanation: Tickets are free, but people still need one.

questions.heading: Message from Josh to Megan
questions.text: Megan, I left my football boots in your brother’s car after training. Could you bring them to school tomorrow? I don’t need them until the match, so don’t make a special journey tonight. Josh
questions.question: Why is Josh contacting Megan?

options.A: To ask her to bring something the next day.
options.B: To arrange extra football training tonight.
options.C: To complain that her brother took his boots.

questions.explanation: Josh asks Megan to bring his boots tomorrow.

questions.heading: Computer Room
questions.text: Students may use the computer room at lunchtime for homework only. Games and videos are not allowed because some students need a quiet place to work. Please leave computers logged off afterwards.
questions.question: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.B: To make sure computers are used for study.
options.A: To invite students to play quiet games.
options.C: To stop students using computers at lunchtime.

questions.explanation: The room is available at lunchtime, but only for homework.

questions.heading: From: Ruby / Subject: Your article
questions.text: I read your article about the new skate park. It’s interesting, but the teacher asked for opinions from different students. Maybe add one or two short comments from people who actually use it.
questions.question: What is Ruby suggesting?

options.C: The article should include other students’ views.
options.A: The article should describe a different skate park.
options.B: The teacher should read the article again.

questions.explanation: Ruby suggests adding comments from people who use the skate park.

questions.heading: Town Market
questions.text: The town market opens every Sunday from 10 a.m. This week, the fruit stalls will be outside the library instead of in Market Street because road repairs are taking place there.
questions.question: What is different about the market this week?

options.A: Some stalls will be in another place.
options.B: The market will open later than usual.
options.C: Fruit will not be sold on Sunday.

questions.explanation: The fruit stalls are moving because of road repairs.
